data.body:
FLIP pushed as Gerrit change 759 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/759). rebar.toml: require_authenticated=true + enforce_since=8dcef776c3dd9e68935b74b82137e1ffd8f04f10, in one change (2026-07-17T03:11:05Z).

ENFORCEMENT IS REAL (demonstrated in isolated temp stores, no shared-store pollution):
- Post-boundary UNSIGNED event -> 'rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated' exits 1 (2 unsigned in scope, enforced). Enforcement is real, not advisory.
- A store with ONLY pre-boundary unsigned events -> exits 0 (correctly grandfathered).
- The local WRITE-GATE refuses an unsignable non-exempt write under require_authenticated (proven by test_identity_txn_signing_heldout.py::test_unsignable_transition_is_refused_under_enforcement, landed in 0ba4).
- The landed regression test test_identity_enforce_since_grandfather.py asserts the same boundary behavior (pre grandfathered / post enforced) via the config/env path.

UNDER-ENFORCEMENT WRITER SURFACES (all sign + verify): local (79da), CI reconcile-bridge (4dc0, event 8b72ddf6), Gerrit review-bot LINK (1a51). CI's Verified gate on change 759 runs verify-identity with require_authenticated=true + the pinned anchor over the whole store — a green Verified is the authoritative confirmation that AC1 holds (all pre-boundary grandfathered, all post-boundary verified) and no writer surface goes red.

ROLLBACK (fast-revert): if a writer surface unexpectedly fails post-flip, set [identity] require_authenticated=false in rebar.toml (leave enforce_since pinned) and land it as a single Gerrit change (push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main, Submit once Verified+1). No data cleanup — the gate returns to advisory immediately. Env override REBAR_IDENTITY_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATED=0 disables it per-run in the interim.
